subject: oop
text: What is a class in C++?
explanation: A class is a blueprint that defines the properties and behaviors of objects.

subject: oop
text: Which of these creates an object of class Dog?
explanation: Declaring a variable of the class type creates an object.

subject: oop
text: What are data members in a class?
explanation: Data members are variables that store the object's state.

subject: oop
options: Functions that belong to a class
explanation: Member functions are functions defined inside a class that operate on objects.

subject: oop
options: Creating an object from a class
explanation: Instantiation is the process of creating an object from a class.

subject: oop
text: Given: class Rectangle { int width; }; What is width?
explanation: width is a data member that stores rectangle's width.

subject: oop
explanation: The 'this' pointer points to the current object instance.

subject: oop
text: What hides internal details of a class?
explanation: Encapsulation bundles data and methods while hiding internal details.

subject: oop
text: What keyword defines a class in C++?
explanation: The 'class' keyword is used to define a class in C++.

subject: oop
text: A cat object has color and weight. What are these?
explanation: Data members store attributes like color and weight.

subject: oop
explanation: Public members are accessible from anywhere in the program.

subject: oop
text: Which specifier restricts access to the defining class only?
explanation: Private members are only accessible within the class.

subject: oop
options: The class and its derived classes
explanation: Protected members are accessible by the class and its derived classes.

subject: oop
explanation: The scope resolution operator :: is used to access members of a particular scope.

subject: oop
text: What is the type of 'this' pointer?
explanation: 'this' is a pointer to the current object, type ClassName*.

subject: oop
text: class Bank { private: double balance; }; Can main() access balance?
explanation: Private members cannot be accessed from outside the class.

subject: oop
text: A friend function can access what?
explanation: Friend functions can access private and protected members.

subject: oop
text: Can a nested class access private members of outer class?
explanation: A nested class can access private members if granted friendship.

subject: oop
text: What is default access for class members?
explanation: Class members are private by default.

subject: oop
text: What is default access for struct members?
explanation: Struct members are public by default.

subject: oop
text: What does a default constructor do?
explanation: A default constructor takes no parameters.

subject: oop
explanation: A parameterized constructor accepts one or more arguments.

subject: oop
explanation: Copy constructor is called in all these situations.

subject: oop
options: When object goes out of scope
explanation: Destructor is called when object goes out of scope.

subject: oop
text: In what order are class members initialized?
explanation: Members are initialized in the order they are declared in the class.

subject: oop
explanation: Base class constructor runs before derived class constructor.

subject: oop
explanation: You can call destructor manually but it's dangerous.

subject: oop
text: Base class members initialize before what?
explanation: Base class members initialize before derived class members.

subject: oop
text: When does constructor of const object run?
explanation: Constructor runs when the const object is declared.

subject: oop
text: When are destructors called for an array of objects?
explanation: Destructors are called in reverse order of creation.

subject: oop
options: Constructs a copy of an object
explanation: Copy constructor creates a new object as a copy of an existing one.

subject: oop
explanation: Assignment operator assigns values to an already existing object.

subject: oop
explanation: Shallow copy copies pointer values, not the data they point to.

subject: oop
explanation: Deep copy allocates new memory and copies the actual data.

subject: oop
explanation: Self-assignment is when an object is assigned to itself.

subject: oop
text: MyClass(const MyClass& obj) is what type of constructor?
explanation: This is a copy constructor - it takes a const reference to another object.

subject: oop
text: What happens if you don't handle self-assignment?
explanation: Self-assignment without handling can cause memory corruption.

subject: oop
text: Rule of Three requires implementing what?
explanation: Rule of Three: destructor, copy constructor, assignment operator.

subject: oop
text: What does = delete mean after a function?
explanation: = delete marks the function as deleted - it cannot be called.

subject: oop
text: What does = default mean for copy constructor?
explanation: = default tells compiler to generate default copy constructor.

subject: oop
text: How many static data members exist per class?
explanation: Static data members are shared across all objects - one per class.

subject: oop
text: What can static member function access directly?
explanation: Static functions can only access static data and other static functions.

subject: oop
text: How do you access static member count without object?
explanation: Static members are accessed via ClassName::member.

subject: oop
options: A function that is part of class
explanation: Friend function is a non-member that can access private members.

subject: oop
explanation: Friend class has full access to private members of the other class.

subject: oop
text: Where is static data member defined?
explanation: Static data members must be defined outside the class.

subject: oop
explanation: Static const members can be initialized inside the class.

subject: oop
explanation: Static functions don't have 'this' pointer.

subject: oop
text: Object obj; obj.staticMethod(); - does this work?
explanation: Static method can be called via object, but not recommended.

subject: oop
explanation: Static variables initialize once at program start.

subject: oop
explanation: Inheritance creates a new class based on an existing class.

subject: oop
explanation: Single inheritance is when one class inherits from one base class.

subject: oop
text: Which allows a class to inherit from multiple classes?
explanation: Multiple inheritance is when a class inherits from more than one class.

subject: oop
explanation: Constructor chaining runs base class constructor before derived constructor.

subject: oop
text: class Dog : public Animal means what?
explanation: Public inheritance means Dog inherits all public members of Animal.

subject: oop
text: In protected inheritance, public becomes what?
explanation: Public members become protected in protected inheritance.

subject: oop
text: Can derived class override base class function?
explanation: Derived can override virtual functions in base class.

subject: oop
options: The class from which others derive
explanation: Base class is the parent class that is inherited from.

subject: oop
explanation: Derived class is a new class created by inheriting from base class.

subject: oop
text: class A {}; class B : public A {}; class C : public B {} is what?
explanation: This is multilevel inheritance - C inherits from B which inherits from A.

subject: oop
explanation: Diamond problem occurs when a class inherits from two classes that both inherit from a common base.

subject: oop
explanation: Virtual inheritance solves diamond problem by having only one copy of the base class.

subject: oop
text: When does ambiguity arise in inheritance?
explanation: Ambiguity occurs when there are multiple inheritance paths to the same base class.

subject: oop
explanation: Use BaseClass::method() to explicitly call the correct version.

subject: oop
text: In virtual inheritance, who initializes virtual base?
explanation: The most derived class is responsible for initializing the virtual base class.

subject: oop
text: virtual void speak() = 0; What does this mean?
explanation: This is a pure virtual function - must be overridden by derived classes.

subject: oop
explanation: A class with pure virtual functions becomes abstract.

subject: oop
text: Can you create object of abstract class?
explanation: Abstract classes cannot be instantiated.

subject: oop
explanation: Using declaration can bring base class members into derived class scope.

subject: oop
explanation: Templates and inheritance work together in C++.

subject: oop
explanation: Function overloading uses same name with different parameters.

subject: oop
explanation: Operator overloading gives new meaning to operators for user-defined types.

subject: oop
explanation: Virtual functions enable runtime polymorphism through override.

subject: oop
explanation: Runtime polymorphism uses virtual functions with base class pointers or references.

subject: oop
explanation: Override ensures the function actually overrides a base virtual function.

subject: oop
text: What does final mean for virtual function?
explanation: Final prevents further overriding of the virtual function.

subject: oop
explanation: Pure virtual has no implementation in the base class.

subject: oop
text: Base* ptr = new Derived(); ptr->foo(); What calls?
explanation: Virtual function calls derived version at runtime - runtime polymorphism.

subject: oop
text: What is difference between override and redefine?
explanation: Override uses virtual keyword for runtime binding, redefine is compile-time.

subject: oop
explanation: Virtual functions can be const in C++.

subject: oop
explanation: Encapsulation hides internal implementation details.

subject: oop
options: Abstract class with pure virtual only
explanation: Interface is an abstract class with only pure virtual functions.

subject: oop
text: How do you declare pure virtual function?
explanation: Pure virtual is declared with = 0;

subject: oop
explanation: Abstract class contains at least one pure virtual function.

subject: oop
explanation: Abstraction hides complexity and shows only essential features.

subject: oop
text: What benefit does abstraction provide to users?
explanation: Users can use functionality without knowing internal implementation.

subject: oop
explanation: Abstraction separates what from how - interface from implementation.

subject: oop
explanation: String is high-level abstraction over char arrays.

subject: oop
text: Good abstraction should provide what to user?
explanation: Good abstraction provides only what user needs to know.

subject: oop
text: Can abstract class have implemented methods?
explanation: Abstract class can have both pure virtual and implemented methods.

subject: oop
explanation: Vtable is a virtual table containing function pointers for dynamic dispatch.

subject: oop
explanation: vptr points to the vtable of the class.

subject: oop
options: Losing derived part when assigned to base
explanation: Object slicing loses derived class parts when assigned to base object.

subject: oop
explanation: Virtual destructor ensures proper cleanup of derived objects.

subject: oop
text: Rule of Three says if you define one of what three, define all three?
explanation: Rule of Three: destructor, copy constructor, copy assignment operator.

subject: oop
text: Rule of Five adds what to Rule of Three?
explanation: Rule of Five adds move constructor and move assignment operator.

subject: oop
explanation: Rule of Zero: use defaults/deleteds for special member functions.

subject: oop
explanation: unique_ptr ensures single ownership - only one can own the resource.

subject: oop
text: shared_ptr uses what to manage ownership?
explanation: shared_ptr uses reference counting to track ownership.

subject: oop
explanation: weak_ptr observes resource owned by shared_ptr without ownership.
